tweets.text:
Remember how I said @CNBC that OpenAI might turn out to be the WeWork of AI? 

It’s getting hard to unload their shares…

cc @carlquintanilla

How can we fight the slop-laden term “agentic” AI before it’s too late?

I fear that it really is about to stick - and historically when that happens you can’t fix it later.

What is your favorite alternative? Or do we just accept it and move on?

NEW paper from Google DeepMind

The biggest threat to AI agents isn't a smarter attacker. It's the web itself.

This work introduces the first systematic framework for understanding how the open web can be weaponized against autonomous agents.

The paper defines "AI Agent Traps": https://t.co/AvKCAceKxm

Always satisfying to visualize improvements as a ladder!

It’s also worth observing here that:
- all methods from 15-72% use dense retrieval models or BM25
- all methods above 80-91% use late interaction models, from LightOn and Mixedbread

A sign that human creativity is a bottleneck is that this year everyone can generate almost any image or video they can think of for nearly free and the April Fools posts are basically just as bad as any other year.

A whole world… built on a table.

- generated on Everlyn in 9.2 seconds https://t.co/sxahcJWDHW

A pre-ChatGPT paper that seems relevant: information processing seems to be the key to growth throughout the long span of recorded history. Better information processing tools are, the paper argues, what keeps societies from collapse.

Presumably Anthropic had fixed the Claude Code bug, so I wanted to give them the benefit of the doubt one more time & compare its rate limits with Codex.
I used this simple prompt for planning and building: “Build a cute pixel RPG to play on the iPhone.”

Both my Claude Max https://t.co/stg9A9JFgL

says a new projection on impacts of AI from @PTetlock and others.

I try not to get cranky about (wrong) AI-generated math slop, but it really is harmful. Our tools for verifying correctness are time-intensive; the  refereeing system relies on good faith of authors. Tempting to take a heads-on-pikes approach to people who take advantage.

Lingshu-Cell

A generative cellular world model for transcriptome modeling toward virtual cells

paper: https://t.co/Axyx7qH9At https://t.co/cLX6WLCrHh

AI data centers are token factories.

See how NVIDIA extreme co-design maximizes token output to profitably scale AI revenue.

🚨Oracle just sent layoff notices to 20,000 to 30,000 employees worldwide in wild 6 AM email.

> 22% revenue gain this quarter
> $50 billion planned AI data center
> lays off ~18% of its global workforce
> stock rises ~6%

It's happening

Most devs think that adding more agents to a planning system should help.

The math says otherwise.

New theoretical work from MIT proves fundamental limits on what multi-agent LLM architectures can achieve.

The work models LLM multi-agent planning as finite acyclic decision https://t.co/yb2dGghhIb

Universal CLAUDE.md

Claims to cut Claude output tokens by 63%!

Drop-in. No code changes.

CLAUDE.md is one of the best ways to steer Claude Code. Not surprised to see the efficiency reported here.

https://t.co/C4x6pVUpND https://t.co/ElbD3kbaa4

The AI labs have actually done a bad job explaining what the future they are building towards will actually look like for most of us.

Even “Machines of Loving Grace” has very few well-articulated visions of what Anthropic hopes life will be like if they succeed at their goals.

Two years ago, we released Gemma, Google DeepMind family of open models.

Today, I'm thrilled to share a new milestone: Gemma 400M downloads and 100,000 variants! 

Thank you to every developer, partner, and contributor. We can't wait to see what you build next!👀 https://t.co/XRZ6tx9q83
